examples.title: Graphics API Pseudocode
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating a simple graphics API
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DrawRectangle
INPUT: x, y, width, height
OUTPUT: rendered rectangle

BEGIN
 // Set up graphics context
 InitializeGraphicsContext()
 
 // Draw rectangle
 DrawLine(x, y, x + width, y)
 DrawLine(x + width, y, x + width, y + height)
 DrawLine(x + width, y + height, x, y + height)
 DrawLine(x, y + height, x, y)
 
 // Clean up graphics context
 CleanUpGraphicsContext()
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates a simple graphics API for drawing a rectangle. It sets up a graphics context, draws the rectangle using four lines, and then cleans up the graphics context.

examples.title: Python Graphics API
examples.description: Python implementation of a simple graphics API
examples.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as patches

# Set up graphics context
fig, ax = plt.subplots()

# Draw rectangle
rect = patches.Rectangle((0.1, 0.1), 0.5, 0.5, linewidth=1, edgecolor='r', facecolor='none')
ax.add_patch(rect)

# Set limits and aspect ratio
ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
ax.set_aspect('equal')

# Show plot
plt.show()
examples.explanation: This Python code uses the matplotlib library to create a simple graphics API for drawing a rectangle. It sets up a graphics context, draws the rectangle, and then displays the plot.
